selection_method.included_set_definition: CVEs listed for the upstream BZIP2 product on the product index, with full record bodies fetched from the MITRE CVE API.

related_but_excluded.summary: zgrep in gzip before 1.3.5 does not properly sanitize arguments, which allows local users to execute arbitrary commands via filenames that are injected into a sed script.
related_but_excluded.reason_excluded_from_upstream_product_se: Listed by Debian for the source package because bzgrep inherited argument-sanitization behavior from zgrep/gzip, but it is not part of the upstream BZIP2 product CVE set used here.

descriptions.value: zgrep in gzip before 1.3.5 does not properly sanitize arguments, which allows local users to execute arbitrary commands via filenames that are injected into a sed script.

cves.component: bzip2 command-line decompression file creation path
cves.notes: Historical CLI overwrite issue; relevant to wrapper/utility behavior, not the libbz2 stream API itself.
cves.summary: bzip2 before 1.0.2 in FreeBSD 4.5 and earlier, OpenLinux 3.1 and 3.1.1, and possibly other operating systems, does not use the O_EXCL flag to create files during decompression and does not warn the user if an existing file would be overwritten, which could allow attackers to overwrite files via a bzip2 archive.

repo_mentions.text: * -f really overwrites files (this was introduced in 1.0.1-1 when we
changed the fopen call to be safer with O_EXCL); closes: #81277.
* Fixed unsafe race condition in opening output files (closes: #56386).

descriptions.value: bzip2 before 1.0.2 in FreeBSD 4.5 and earlier, OpenLinux 3.1 and 3.1.1, and possibly other operating systems, does not use the O_EXCL flag to create files during decompression and does not warn the user if an existing file would be overwritten, which could allow attackers to overwrite files via a bzip2 archive.

description_data.value: bzip2 before 1.0.2 in FreeBSD 4.5 and earlier, OpenLinux 3.1 and 3.1.1, and possibly other operating systems, does not use the O_EXCL flag to create files during decompression and does not warn the user if an existing file would be overwritten, which could allow attackers to overwrite files via a bzip2 archive.

cves.component: bzip2 command-line decompression temporary permissions handling
cves.notes: Historical CLI file-permission race during decompression; not a core library memory-safety issue.
cves.summary: Race condition in bzip2 before 1.0.2 in FreeBSD 4.5 and earlier, OpenLinux 3.1 and 3.1.1, and possibly other operating systems, decompresses files with world-readable permissions before setting the permissions to what is specified in the bzip2 archive, which could allow local users to read the files as they are being decompressed.

repo_mentions.text: * Avoid race condition between creating the output file and setting its
interim permissions safely, by using fopen_output_safely().
* Fixed unsafe race condition in opening output files (closes: #56386).

descriptions.value: Race condition in bzip2 before 1.0.2 in FreeBSD 4.5 and earlier, OpenLinux 3.1 and 3.1.1, and possibly other operating systems, decompresses files with world-readable permissions before setting the permissions to what is specified in the bzip2 archive, which could allow local users to read the files as they are being decompressed.

description_data.value: Race condition in bzip2 before 1.0.2 in FreeBSD 4.5 and earlier, OpenLinux 3.1 and 3.1.1, and possibly other operating systems, decompresses files with world-readable permissions before setting the permissions to what is specified in the bzip2 archive, which could allow local users to read the files as they are being decompressed.

cves.component: bzip2 command-line file permission handling around symlinks
cves.notes: Historical upstream product issue outside the libbz2 in-memory API surface.
cves.summary: bzip2 before 1.0.2 in FreeBSD 4.5 and earlier, OpenLinux 3.1 and 3.1.1, and possibly systems, uses the permissions of symbolic links instead of the actual files when creating an archive, which could cause the files to be extracted with less restrictive permissions than intended.

repo_mentions.text: * Dereference symlinks when copying file permissions in -f mode.

descriptions.value: bzip2 before 1.0.2 in FreeBSD 4.5 and earlier, OpenLinux 3.1 and 3.1.1, and possibly systems, uses the permissions of symbolic links instead of the actual files when creating an archive, which could cause the files to be extracted with less restrictive permissions than intended.

description_data.value: bzip2 before 1.0.2 in FreeBSD 4.5 and earlier, OpenLinux 3.1 and 3.1.1, and possibly systems, uses the permissions of symbolic links instead of the actual files when creating an archive, which could cause the files to be extracted with less restrictive permissions than intended.

cves.notes: Hard-link race affecting output file permission changes after decompression.
cves.summary: Race condition in bzip2 1.0.2 and earlier allows local users to modify permissions of arbitrary files via a hard link attack on a file while it is being decompressed, whose permissions are changed by bzip2 after the decompression is complete.

repo_mentions.text: * Fix file permissions race problem (CAN-2005-0953).
- Fix file permissions race problem (CAN-2005-0953).
* Fixed RC bug "file permissions modification race (CAN-2005-0953)", closes:

descriptions.value: Race condition in bzip2 1.0.2 and earlier allows local users to modify permissions of arbitrary files via a hard link attack on a file while it is being decompressed, whose permissions are changed by bzip2 after the decompression is complete.

description_data.value: Race condition in bzip2 1.0.2 and earlier allows local users to modify permissions of arbitrary files via a hard link attack on a file while it is being decompressed, whose permissions are changed by bzip2 after the decompression is complete.

cves.notes: Affects decompression behavior and is directly relevant to a safe Rust port.
cves.summary: bzip2 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (hard drive consumption) via a crafted bzip2 file that causes an infinite loop (a.k.a "decompression bomb").

repo_mentions.text: to 1.0.3 is recommended.  This fixes CAN-2005-1260.
* Fixed "CAN-2005-1260 decompression bomb vulnerability", closes: #310803.

descriptions.value: bzip2 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (hard drive consumption) via a crafted bzip2 file that causes an infinite loop (a.k.a "decompression bomb").

cves.notes: Directly affects libbz2 parsing logic in the library code.
cves.summary: bzlib.c in bzip2 before 1.0.5 allows user-assisted remote attackers to cause a denial of service (crash) via a crafted file that triggers a buffer over-read, as demonstrated by the PROTOS GENOME test suite for Archive Formats.

descriptions.value: bzlib.c in bzip2 before 1.0.5 allows user-assisted remote attackers to cause a denial of service (crash) via a crafted file that triggers a buffer over-read, as demonstrated by the PROTOS GENOME test suite for Archive Formats.

references.name: 20081203 VMSA-2008-0019 VMware Hosted products and patches for ESX and ESXi resolve a critical security issue and update bzip2

description_data.value: bzlib.c in bzip2 before 1.0.5 allows user-assisted remote attackers to cause a denial of service (crash) via a crafted file that triggers a buffer over-read, as demonstrated by the PROTOS GENOME test suite for Archive Formats.

reference_data.name: 20081203 VMSA-2008-0019 VMware Hosted products and patches for ESX and ESXi resolve a critical security issue and update bzip2

references.name: 20081203 VMSA-2008-0019 VMware Hosted products and patches for ESX and ESXi resolve a critical security issue and update bzip2

cves.notes: Directly affects integer arithmetic and bounds handling in the library decompressor.
cves.summary: Integer overflow in the BZ2_decompress function in decompress.c in bzip2 and libbzip2 before 1.0.6 allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly execute arbitrary code via a crafted compressed file.

repo_mentions.text: * Security fix for CVE-2010-0405.  This was reported by Mikolaj
* Fix integer overflow
http://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2010-0405

descriptions.value: Integer overflow in the BZ2_decompress function in decompress.c in bzip2 and libbzip2 before 1.0.6 allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly execute arbitrary code via a crafted compressed file.

references.name: 20101207 VMSA-2010-0019 VMware ESX third party updates for Service Console

description_data.value: Integer overflow in the BZ2_decompress function in decompress.c in bzip2 and libbzip2 before 1.0.6 allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly execute arbitrary code via a crafted compressed file.

reference_data.name: 20101207 VMSA-2010-0019 VMware ESX third party updates for Service Console

references.name: 20101207 VMSA-2010-0019 VMware ESX third party updates for Service Console

cves.notes: Affects the bundled bzexe helper script, not the libbz2 compression/decompression engine.
cves.summary: The bzexe command in bzip2 1.0.5 and earlier generates compressed executables that do not properly handle temporary files during extraction, which allows local users to execute arbitrary code by precreating a temporary directory.

repo_mentions.text: * Fix "insecure temporary file creation (bzexe)"

descriptions.value: The bzexe command in bzip2 1.0.5 and earlier generates compressed executables that do not properly handle temporary files during extraction, which allows local users to execute arbitrary code by precreating a temporary directory.

references.name: [oss-security] 20111028 Re: Request for CVE Identifier: bzexe insecure	temporary  file
[oss-security] 20111028 Re: Request for CVE Identifier: bzexe insecure	temporary  file

cves.notes: Targets the bundled recovery utility rather than the library, but still lives in the upstream source tree.
cves.summary: Use-after-free vulnerability in bzip2recover in bzip2 1.0.6 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (crash) via a crafted bzip2 file, related to block ends set to before the start of the block.

repo_mentions.text: * bzip2recover: Fix use after free issue with outFile (CVE-2016-3189)
- bzip2recover-CVE-2016-3189.patch
* bzip2recover: Fix potential use-after-free, Closes: #827744 (CVE-2016-3189)

descriptions.value: Use-after-free vulnerability in bzip2recover in bzip2 1.0.6 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (crash) via a crafted bzip2 file, related to block ends set to before the start of the block.

cves.notes: Direct out-of-bounds write in the decompressor; high-priority reference for the Rust port.
cves.summary: BZ2_decompress in decompress.c in bzip2 through 1.0.6 has an out-of-bounds write when there are many selectors.

repo_mentions.text: * Make sure nSelectors is not out of range (CVE-2019-12900)
This relaxes the fix for CVE-2019-12900 from 1.0.7
* Make sure nSelectors is not out of range (CVE-2019-12900)

descriptions.value: BZ2_decompress in decompress.c in bzip2 through 1.0.6 has an out-of-bounds write when there are many selectors.
